question_title: As of April 30, 2026, what will be the cumulative number of people detected arriving in the UK in small boats in 2026?
summary: UK small boat arrivals for Jan 1 - Apr 30, 2026. As of March 4, 2026, the cumulative total is ~2,614, running 27% ahead of 2025's pace (~2,056 at the same point). In 2025, the 10,000 mark was reached before end of April, putting Jan-Apr 2025 at roughly 10,000-12,000. Monte Carlo simulations using two approaches (day-by-day weather model and 2025-benchmark scaling) both produce a median around 11,500 for Jan-Apr 2026. The key uncertainty is weather in March-April; the 10,000 threshold between the two most likely buckets sits near the simulation median, creating genuine two-way risk. The upward annual trend (36.8k→41k) and 2026 running ahead of pace favor 10,001-20,000 as the most likely outcome.

factors.description: 2026 running ~27% ahead of 2025 pace at early March (2,614 vs 2,056), suggesting higher Jan-Apr total than 2025's ~10,000-12,000
2025 Jan-Apr total was ~10,000-12,000 (10,000 reached 'before end of April' per Migration Watch), providing a strong baseline near the critical 10k threshold
Overall upward annual trend: 36.8k (2024) → 41k (2025), with media reporting 2026 is 'on track to be record-breaking'
Weather uncertainty: March-April crossings are highly weather-dependent with many zero-days. Extended storms could keep total under 10,000
factors.conditional: If March-April has unusually bad weather

factors.description: Government enforcement measures (French pilot deal, £100M border tech) could reduce crossings, though historical track record of enforcement reducing numbers is poor
Already at 2,614 through Mar 4 — reaching only 5,000 by Apr 30 would require near-zero crossings over 57 prime-season days, which is extremely unlikely
20,001+ would require unprecedented Jan-Apr volume. Even record year 2022 (~46k annual) likely had only ~11,500 in Jan-Apr. No historical precedent for this level
Monte Carlo simulation (two models averaged) produces median of ~11,500 with 10-90th percentile range of ~7,000-17,500, placing central estimate solidly in the 10,001-20,000 bucket

reasoning: The reflection is approved. Let me now produce my final forecast.

resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the option whose range contains the cumulative total number of people detected arriving in the UK in small boats from January 1, 2026 through April 30, 2026 (inclusive), as recorded in the Home Office small boats time series dataset available before May 1, 2026. The primary source is the Home Office dataset file at https://assets.publishing.service.gov.uk/media/6793689a5dc1be141475ff84/small_boats_time_series.ods.
fine_print: Compute the cumulative total by summing the daily “arrivals” (people detected arriving) values for all dates from January 1, 2026 through April 30, 2026 inclusive, using the values in the primary dataset as available before May 1, 2026. If the dataset is revised after May 1, 2026, ignore those later revisions. If the primary dataset is unavailable at resolution time, resolve using credible sources per https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions. Candidate Sources: UK Home Office small boat activity statistics landing page at https://www.gov.uk/government/statistics/small-boat-activity
background_info:
Cumulative small boat arrivals are a high-salience operational and political metric for UK border policy, asylum processing capacity, and enforcement strategy.
